extra.reasoning:
The property (never rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true) holds across the entire input space.

rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires the exact conjunction C = (hypertension AND g6pd_deficiency AND peptic_ulcer AND diabetes AND hepatic_impairment). Otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false.

rec_drug_b has two rules: guard_suppress_b (priority 90) sets rec_drug_b := false under exactly the same conjunction C; rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true when hypertension.

The critical interaction: the guard's antecedent is identical to rec_a's antecedent. Whenever rec_drug_a becomes true, all five conditions of C hold, so guard_suppress_b also fires. Its priority 90 beats rec_b_from_trigger's priority 50, forcing rec_drug_b := false. Therefore rec_drug_a = true implies rec_drug_b = false in every case.

When C is false, rec_drug_a is false (its only rule doesn't fire, default applies), so the conjunction (a AND b) cannot hold regardless of rec_drug_b. The renal_impairment and polypharmacy rules only affect rec_drug_c (priority 40) and are irrelevant to this property.

Hence no input assignment makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true. The property holds.
